vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #38

plan.title: Make the time display easier to read: move it to the temperature row, right-aligned, matching size

narratives.Description: Make the time display easier to read: move it to the temperature row, right-aligned, matching size
narratives.Overview:
## Request
The time display should be easier for motorcyclists to read at a glance.

- **Move it down** to the same line/row as the **temperature** display.
- **Right-align** it (all the way to the right side of that row).
- **Match the temperature display's font and size**.

## Current behavior
- The clock lives in the **brand header** (`AppClockView` inside `BrandHeaderView`), top-right next to the GPS signal status. It's small: `.font(.caption.weight(.semibold))`, `monospacedDigit()`, in `palette.secondaryColor`.
- The temperature lives in the top bar (`WeatherBadgeView` inside `RideView.topBar`), top-left, rendered at `.font(.system(size: 32, weight: .semibold, design: .rounded))` with `monospacedDigit()`.

## Desired behavior
- Place the time on the temperature row in `RideView.topBar` (temperature left, time pushed to the far right via the existing `Spacer()`).
- Style the time to match the temperature: `.system(size: 32, weight: .semibold, design: .rounded)`, `monospacedDigit()`.
- Remove the clock from `BrandHeaderView` (or relocate accordingly) so it isn't shown twice.

## Notes
- Confirm color: match the temperature's neutral color (e.g. `palette.primaryColor`/`secondaryColor`) rather than the temperature-warning tint.
narratives.FunctionalRequirements:
FR-1: The time MUST appear on the same row as the temperature in RideView.topBar, pushed to the far right.
FR-2: The time MUST use the temperature's font/size: .system(size: 32, weight: .semibold, design: .rounded) with monospacedDigit().
FR-3: The clock MUST no longer appear in BrandHeaderView (no duplicate).
FR-4: The time color MUST use a neutral palette color (not the temperature-warning tint).
narratives.Architecture: Move AppClockView out of BrandHeaderView's trailing slot. In RideView.topBar, after the existing Spacer(), render AppClockView with a larger style. Add a font/size to AppClockView (parameterize or restyle) so it matches the temperature; keep TimelineView-based minute updates and the accessibility label. Verify BrandHeaderView's HStack still lays out sensibly with only the GPS status (Spacer retained).
narratives.TestingStrategy: Build + xcodebuild test (no new logic to unit-test; this is layout/styling). On-device visual check that time is right-aligned on the temperature row at matching size across Day/Night/Amber, and absent from the header.

items.title: Relocate clock to temperature row at matching size

narrative.Acceptance: Time on temp row, right-aligned, 32pt rounded, removed from header.

references.title: Issue #38: Make the time display easier to read: move it to the temperature row, right-aligned, matching size
